# kino

> A themeable, keyboard-first React video player with pluggable providers (Mux HLS, native files, YouTube, Vimeo, audio-driven scene sequences) under one glass UI. MIT licensed.

## Docs

- [Overview](https://kino.karnstack.com/): what kino is, live playground, why kino.
- [Install & API](https://kino.karnstack.com/install): install, quick start, the provider/props API.
- [Providers](https://kino.karnstack.com/providers): the Provider contract and the five shipped providers.
- [Scene protocol](https://kino.karnstack.com/scene-protocol): the sequence manifest and the postMessage wire format between the embedding player and the iframe host.
- [Theming](https://kino.karnstack.com/theming): CSS custom properties, keyboard map, capability gating.

## Scenes

- [Scenes demo](https://kino.karnstack.com/scenes.html): an audio-driven React scene sequence played through kino's chrome. The audio file is the master clock, a manifest maps time ranges onto React scene modules, and every frame is resolution-independent DOM.
- [Scenes host page](https://kino.karnstack.com/scenes-host.html): the iframe host that owns the audio element and the scene DOM, built with createSceneHost.

## Reference

- [npm: @karnstack/kino](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@karnstack/kino): entry points `@karnstack/kino` (core + MuxPlayer), `/mux`, `/native`, `/youtube`, `/vimeo`, `/scenes`, `/styles.css`.
- [GitHub](https://github.com/karnstack/kino)
- [README](https://github.com/karnstack/kino/blob/main/README.md)
